articles.title: AI is hurting Apple in more ways than one: it may force iPhone price increases
articles.description: CEO Tim Cook said in a recent interview that the situation is "unsustainable."

articles.title: Chi-Hua Chien saw Facebook coming — now he says the real AI winners won’t be selling AI
articles.description: Chi-Hua Chien has spent more than two decades as a venture capitalist, but he thinks like a cultural anthropologist.

articles.title: Roelof Botha joins SpaceX’s board of directors
articles.description: The former Sequoia Capital leader is filling an "existing vacancy" on SpaceX's board, days after the company went public in the largest IPO ever.

articles.title: After unveiling ridiculously expensive AR glasses, Snap’s stock takes a dive
articles.description: Snap's long-awaited smart glasses debut hasn't exactly done wonders for the company's stock.

articles.title: NEA’s Tiffany Luck says enterprises are still figuring out their AI ROI
articles.description: Tokenmaxxing was the hottest trend in Silicon Valley earlier this year, with CEOs encouraging employees to push AI usage as far as it would go. Then the bill came due. Uber reportedly blew through its annual AI budget in a few months, some companies cut Claude licenses for parts of their org, and Meta killed its internal leaderboard.  This tension between […]

articles.title: FTC lawsuit reveals how subscription scam networks evade app store enforcement
articles.description: A new FTC lawsuit reveals how sophisticated subscription app operators can allegedly use shell companies and payment infrastructure to stay active on app stores despite mounting consumer complaints.

articles.title: World leaders want American AI. They just don’t want America to be able to turn it off.
articles.description: French President Macron and Indian PM Modi raised alarms at the G7 summit that the U.S. could cut off access to American AI overnight — a fear the Anthropic blackout just made real.

articles.title: Anthropic becomes first AI startup to join the Frontier carbon removal coalition
articles.description: Anthropic has joined the Frontier coalition, which received another $915M in pledges to fund carbon removal projects.

articles.title: Cybercriminals allegedly hacked tens of thousands of Fortinet firewalls used by major companies all over the world
articles.description: An alleged Russian-speaking group of cybercriminals is reportedly compromising and targeting several major companies that use Fortinet Firewalls and VPNs through previously known passwords.

articles.title: Social media’s next evolution: user-controlled algorithms
articles.description: Social media feeds are becoming more customizable as platforms like Threads, Instagram, and TikTok introduce tools that let users directly influence the algorithms powering their recommendations.

articles.title: World model maker Odyssey nabs $1.45B valuation backed by Amazon and other big names
articles.description: World models are the next big thing in AI beyond LLMs and, with this round, Odyssey has cemented itself as one of the startups to watch.

articles.title: Mastodon looks to newsletters to help revive the open social web
articles.description: Mastodon’s newly launched newsletter feature lets anyone subscribe to creators by email, even without a Mastodon account.

articles.title: Only 16 percent of Americans think AI will have a positive impact on society, a new study shows
articles.description: Although Wall Street loves AI, every day Americans are significantly less optimistic about the industry, a new report from Pew Research shows.

articles.title: Two Stanford grads raise $11M to build a noninvasive wearable for hormone tracking
articles.description: Clair Health will track inflammation and bloating markers, energy levels, and cycle phase classification to give insights into cycle irregularities and perimenopause, as well as hormonal fluctuations, and how to navigate those changes.

articles.title: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
articles.description: Google is betting generative AI can breathe new life into the smart speaker. The company's new $99.99 Google Home Speaker replaces the rigid commands of the Google Assistant era with more conversational Gemini interactions.

articles.title: PayPal Ventures shutters as company restructuring continues
articles.description: The corporate venture arm ends after 10 years and 80 investments.

articles.title: The slowtech revolution is here to kill your phone addiction and rescue your attention span
articles.description: “People just really want to take back control of their time, their lives, their attention... They’re down for whatever helps them do that.”

articles.title: Collecting robot training data is dirty, unglamorous work. Some AI labs are already paying XDOF to do it.
articles.description: If physical AI is going to match the accomplishments of LLMs, there's a data problem that needs to be solved.

articles.title: Pramaana Labs raises $27M seed round from Khosla Ventures to bring formal verification to AI
articles.description: Pramaana will focus on highly sensitive verticals like law, drug discovery, and tax preparation — where errors can be costly and reliability is at a premium.

articles.title: SpaceX alum nabs $22M to turn rocket engines into geothermal power plants
articles.description: Critical Energy is turning rocket engines into geothermal power plants, and it wants to build 300 GW per year by 2045.

articles.title: Second carcass-eating fly species cleared by FDA for maggot wound therapy
articles.description: Maggot therapy lacks robust data, but it has fans and a fail-safe "bacon therapy."

articles.title: Sooner than expected? Useful quantum error correction promised for 2028.
articles.description: Elsewhere, beyond-classical quantum hardware, plus classical computing fires back.

articles.title: Tesco moving 40,000 server workloads off VMware amid Broadcom's “abusive conduct”
articles.description: Tesco claimed Broadcom hiked its VMware prices by about 175 percent in UK court filings.

articles.title: "Dangerous" AI models are coming no matter what

articles.title: Ten months later, the $100 Google Home Speaker is finally available for preorder
articles.description: Google's new smart speaker is more about Gemini than audio quality.

tech.summary:
今日科技动态摘要

1. **AI对苹果的负面影响**：AI技术可能迫使iPhone涨价，从而对苹果公司造成多方面冲击。  
2. **AI行业的赢家定位**：风险投资人Chi-Hua Chien指出，真正的AI赢家将不是直接出售AI技术的公司。  
3. **企业AI投资回报率问题**：NEA的Tiffany Luck表示，企业仍在探索如何衡量AI的投资回报率。  
4. **Snap高价AR眼镜引发股价下跌**：Snap发布价格昂贵的AR眼镜后，股价随之大幅下跌。  
5. **全球对美AI的依赖与顾虑**：世界领导人渴望使用美国AI技术，但担忧美国能随时关闭这些系统。  

今日最值得关注的发展：AI正从多个层面重塑科技行业格局，不仅影响苹果等巨头的定价策略，也引发企业对投资回报率的重新思考，同时全球对AI技术的依赖与安全顾虑并存。

ai.summary:
今日AI动态摘要

1. **多模态与机器人技术进展**：MolmoMotion实现语言引导的3D运动预测，Strands Agents和LeRobot将Hugging Face模型部署至真实机器人硬件，推动AI从虚拟到物理世界的应用。
2. **长时任务与智能代理**：GLM-5.2专为长周期任务优化，Agentic Resource Discovery让AI代理自主搜索资源，提升复杂场景下的自主决策能力。
3. **AI在科学和医疗领域的突破**：AMIE医疗AI可辅助管理健康状况，OpenAI的“近自主化学家”改进药物合成反应，同时推出LifeSciBench评估模型在生命科学中的表现。
4. **模型行为预测与生态建设**：OpenAI通过模拟部署预测模型发布前的行为，并推出合作伙伴网络与学院课程，加速AI应用落地与人才培养。
5. **社区与能源投资**：Google在亚拉巴马州和弗吉尼亚州新增投资，支持当地就业与能源可负担性，体现科技企业在地化发展策略。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：AI正从实验室走向真实世界——无论是机器人硬件部署、药物合成自动化，还是医疗健康管理，多模态、长周期自主决策与行业垂直应用成为当前最活跃的突破方向。

cctv.summary:
今日新闻联播动态摘要

1. **中国批驳菲律宾与日本划界言论**：中方专家指出，菲律宾外长声称日菲海上划界“与中国无关”的说法站不住脚，警告此举背后有超越双边关系的别有用意。
2. **中国推动AI普惠与发展**：中国向发展中国家免费提供人工智能技术，而G7国家则聚焦于谁有资格使用美国模型，凸显技术发展路径差异。
3. **“小红书”赴港IPO估值超700亿美元**：被称为“中国版Instagram”的社交平台准备在香港上市，估值有望突破700亿美元，引发市场关注。
4. **中国学术诚信风波**：“学生耿”事件引发对中国科研诚信的广泛讨论，年轻学者公开质疑资深教授学术不端行为。
5. **中美科技与贸易摩擦持续**：美国要求追回已售予中国的军用电视墙制造商；福特等企业因美国联网汽车新规寻求中国产车型许可；中国禁止销售德国英飞凌氮化镓芯片，并对磷化铟出口加强管控。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：中国在人工智能、新能源（菲律宾成中国第二大太阳能板市场）及核能领域持续发力，同时通过出口管制和反制措施应对西方技术封锁，展现出自主创新与战略博弈并重的姿态。
